nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brugge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch (Van der Brugge): topographic name for someone living near a brug ‘bridge’.
Flemish and Dutch: habitational name from Bruges.
North German (Brügge): variant of Bruck or a habitational name from any of several places called Brügge.

variants.name: Bruck
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Naftali, Eliahu, Hershel, Meyer, Ruvim, Shloime, Yoav.

German (also Brück): topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge, or an occupational name for a bridge keeper or toll collector on a bridge, from Middle High German bruck(e) ‘bridge’. Bruck remained the Bavarian and Austrian form, reflected in placenames such as Innsbruck, whereas in central and northern Germany Brück(e) was the regular and ultimately standard form, as for example in the placename Osnabrück. Switzerland has both forms. See also Brueck .
Jewish (from Poland and Belarus): from Polish, Belorussian, or Yiddish bruk ‘pavement’, possibly a metonymic occupational name for a paver.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John De Brugge was born in 1368, in Ross, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Baldwin De Brugge, was 40 and his mother, Isabel Elizabeth Grandison, was 32. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Joan Pyne. He died on 30 September 1436, in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 68.

Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Joan Pyne. Il est décédé le 30 septembre 1436, à Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John De Brugge(이)가 1368년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 로스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baldwin De Brugge(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Elizabeth Grandison(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Joan Pyne(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1436년 9월 30일 영국 허퍼드실르 로스--w예에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
